name: Whoosh (Pure Python Full-Text Search)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-2); Matt Chaput (archived — no active development since 2015)

auth.notes: Local library; index stored on filesystem

performance.notes: Pure Python; no native binaries; adequate for small-medium corpora

agent_readiness.notes: Pure Python full-text search library. from whoosh import index, fields, qparser. schema = fields.Schema(title=fields.TEXT(stored=True), path=fields.ID(stored=True), content=fields.TEXT). ix = index.create_in('indexdir', schema). writer = ix.writer(). writer.add_document(title='Hello', path='/a', content='First document'). writer.commit(). with ix.searcher() as s: results = s.search(qparser.QueryParser('content', ix.schema).parse('hello')). BM25F ranking by default. Supports phrase queries, wildcards, faceting, highlighting. FileStorage, RamStorage. ix.doc_count(). Results pagination. Spell correction. Stored fields for retrieval.

gotchas: Archived project — no updates since 2015; Python 3.11+ may have issues
Pure Python — slow on large corpora (>100K docs); use Elasticsearch or Lucene
Concurrent writes not supported — single writer lock
No type annotations — poor IDE support
Stored fields bloat index size if large content stored
best_when: Small to medium corpora needing full-text search without external services
Embedded search in desktop apps or offline tools
Simple keyword search with BM25F ranking and faceting
avoid_when: Large corpora (>100K docs) — use Elasticsearch or Manticore Search
Production systems requiring active maintenance and security patches
When semantic search is needed (use txtai or LanceDB)
summary: Whoosh is a pure Python full-text search library (archived, BSD-2). Schema → writer.add_document() → commit() → searcher.search(). BM25F ranking. Faceting, highlighting, spell correction. Filesystem-based index. No external deps. Good for small embedded search needs. Archived since 2015 — no active development. Single writer lock. Slow on large corpora. For new projects prefer Meilisearch or Elasticsearch.
